item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Trying to destroy the world is a perfectly natural reaction to being fully aware of what living in the SCP foundation universe actually means.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the "Discovery Notes" section, shouldn't it say "lost as casualties" instead of "lost as causalities"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think it's an anomalous effect driving people nuts - I think it's just existential despair at seeing so many different ways for the world to end, without having time for coping/acceptance to kick in. Convince people that they're doomed, and they'll fight back; convince them that there is no fight possible, and they'll sink into apathy. Give them a way out, that seems less horrible than the alternative..?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems simultaneously too useful and yet another "sends you insane" skip. Apocalypse-saturation left me disinterested.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Ah, my bad. The image for this is entirely created by myself, hand drawn and then photographed.

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page. If the images are not wholly your own, please post the image's source as a link (or links) in reply to this post and edit it into your first post in this discussion thread.

Edit: Problem resolved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This sort of reminds me of that book that gives its readers false knowledge about an imminent apocalypse where they're the Chosen One, except this one seems to convince them that actually bringing about one of the apocalypses is a good thing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had considered creating a separate page for it, but I'm not entirely sure I'm going to do much else with it - right now I can't see much beyond just logs documenting each scenario. And obvious what's there right now isn't enough to justify its own page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Though, if you have more plans for it, consider making it a separate piece (something a little more substantial than what's contained in the addenda here though) as daveyyoufool does with SCP-2922.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pacing could use some tweaking as well. The Project Theia was a dreadfully boring note to end off on. It's typical of what one would expect given what's been presented this far, I think it'd be better as something referenced throughout or alluded to.

Agreeing with this notion. Considering that this is classed Thaumiel from the get-go, committing to the idea throughout would be stronger. Instead, it presented some level of risk that is ultimately false – if it's classed Thaumiel from the start, it is implied that the Foundation troubleshot the risk or a sign of the wrong object class being used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I actually agree with having the recovery log last, now that you mention it. I'll move that to the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hesitant upvote. It's quite well-written for the most part, and the various logs give a well-rounded look of the anomaly, but The dialogue almost earned a downvote all its own. It's leaning too far towards ham at the moment. I think it could use a tone-down to be a little more…reserved, perhaps?

Pacing could use some tweaking as well. The Project Theia was a dreadfully boring note to end off on. It's typical of what one would expect given what's been presented this far, I think it'd be better as something referenced throughout or alluded to. I feel like the interview log and recovery treanscript should be swapped, as the recov log has just the right amount of 'oh fuck' to leave off on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this drags on for too long but I enjoyed it enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Intriguing! Definitely a very original premise. Reminds me of the Daeva a little. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, you can make an author page now! Also the SCP is great too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my third skip! Been working on this one for a while and it's gone through a lot of revisions and changes since my initial idea.
Thanks to everyone who read it in all its various forms in chat and on the forum, especially Lazar Lyusternik and Zachary Maxwell.

The image used for this is entirely created by myself, hand drawn and then photographed.
